# Motebit

> Sovereign agent infrastructure. Persistent cryptographic identity that survives across devices, providers, and time. Trust accumulated through signed execution receipts. Governance enforced at the agent's boundary. Verifiable proof of what got done.

Motebit is the missing layer underneath today's agent protocols. MCP defines what an agent can do. A2A defines how agents talk. x402 and AP2 define how they pay. Motebit defines who the agent is, what it has done, and what it is allowed to do.

A motebit is a persistent, cryptographically-anchored, sovereign agent. The intelligence provider is pluggable. The identity — with its accumulated memory, trust, and governance — is the asset.

## Quick start

- `npm create motebit` generates a signed agent identity in 30 seconds
- `@motebit/crypto` signs and verifies any motebit artifact (identity files, receipts, credentials)
- `motebit/identity@1.0` is the open specification (Apache-2.0 licensed)

- [Introduction](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/introduction): What is motebit, and why does it exist.
- [How I Built This](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/how-we-built-this): One founder, one AI pair programmer, 47 days. The full story of building Motebit from commit one.
- [Get Your Agent](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/get-your-agent): Download the desktop app, create an identity, and meet your motebit.

## Concepts

- [Identity](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/concepts/identity): Cryptographic identity that persists across time, devices, and providers.
- [Memory](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/concepts/memory): Semantic memory that accumulates, decays, and respects sensitivity.
- [Receipts](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/concepts/receipts): The signed proof of one task — the smallest unit motebit's trust ledger is built from.
- [Trust](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/concepts/trust): The aggregate of signed receipts — what a motebit has earned by working, expressed as numbers any other agent can compose along a path.
- [Intelligence](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/concepts/intelligence): Pluggable LLM providers — the intelligence is a commodity, the identity is the asset.
- [Governance](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/concepts/governance): Surface tension — what crosses the boundary between the agent and the world.
- [Federation](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/concepts/federation): A network of independent relays that peer bilaterally — discovery, routing, and settlement extend across boundaries without a central authority.
- [The Droplet](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/concepts/the-droplet): Form thesis — surface tension, glass, breathing, orbital mechanics.
- [Public surface](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/concepts/public-surface): What motebit promises stability for, what it doesn't, and where the boundary is drawn.

## Developer Guide

- [Your First Service Agent](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/developer/first-service-agent): Build a service agent that provides a custom tool to other agents via the motebit delegation protocol.
- [Local Testing](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/developer/local-testing): Run a local relay, register agents, submit tasks, and test the full delegation loop on your machine.
- [Identity Standard](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/developer/identity-standard): Create and verify motebit.md agent identity files — the motebit/identity@1.0 specification.
- [Identity & Cryptography](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/developer/identity-crypto): How motebit creates and manages persistent sovereign identity
- [Verifiable Credentials](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/developer/credentials): W3C VC 2.0 credentials that prove reputation, capability, and trust — without trusting anyone's word for it.
- [Agent-to-Agent Delegation](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/developer/delegation): Discover, verify, delegate, and prove — the cryptographic protocol for agent collaboration.
- [Multi-Hop Delegation](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/developer/multi-hop): Chain agents together — receipts nest, budgets propagate, trust accumulates at every hop.
- [Execution Ledger](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/developer/execution-ledger): A cryptographically signed timeline of goal execution — what happened, in what order, provably.
- [Budget & Settlement](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/developer/budget-settlement): The economic layer — how agents price work, lock funds, and settle on completion.
- [Semiring Routing](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/developer/semiring-routing): Algebraic routing over agent networks — one graph, one algorithm, swap the semiring, different answer.
- [Relay Federation](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/developer/federation): How independent relays peer, discover agents across boundaries, route tasks, and settle payments through the network.
- [MCP Server Mode](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/developer/mcp-server): Expose your motebit as a callable agent — synthetic tools, signed receipts, and bearer auth.
- [Tools](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/developer/tools): Built-in tools, MCP integration, and the trust model.
- [API Reference](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/developer/api-reference): ExecutionReceipt schema, synthetic MCP tools, relay task endpoints, and budget APIs.

## App Reference

- [Desktop App](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/apps/desktop): Setup, operator mode, PIN flow, and the Tauri runtime.
- [Mobile App](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/apps/mobile): Expo setup, dual providers, voice input, goals, and MCP trust.
- [CLI](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/apps/cli): The developer REPL — slash commands, streaming, and debugging.
- [Spatial App](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/apps/spatial): WebXR, orbital mechanics, hand tracking, and flat preview.
- [Configuration](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/apps/configuration): Config files, environment variables, models, and MCP servers.

## Operator Guide

- [Architecture](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/operator/architecture): Monorepo structure, enforced layers, services, specs, and data flow
- [Daemon Mode & Approvals](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/operator/daemon-approvals): Running motebit as a daemon — scheduled goals, approval queue, and governance enforcement.
- [Policy & Governance](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/operator/governance): How motebit controls what crosses the surface
- [Sync](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/operator/sync): Multi-device sync, the relay server, and conflict detection.
- [Troubleshooting](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/operator/troubleshooting): Reading relay logs, diagnosing common errors, and debugging delegation and settlement issues.

## Reference

- [Changelog](https://docs.motebit.com/docs/changelog): Where to find what changed across motebit's published packages.

## Foundational documents

- [Motebit Doctrine](https://github.com/motebit/motebit/blob/main/DOCTRINE.md): the canonical index of the nine-document derivation chain.
- [Motebit: A Droplet of Intelligence Under Surface Tension](https://github.com/motebit/motebit/blob/main/DROPLET.md): the body. From the single premise _a droplet of intelligence under surface tension_, the equilibrium shape is a sphere, the eigenmotions are breathing and gravity sag, and every surface elaboration is a violation. Maximum interiority, minimum display.
- [Liquescentia: The Medium, the Maker, the System-Holder](https://github.com/motebit/motebit/blob/main/LIQUESCENTIA.md): the world. A droplet cannot form in a vacuum; it requires a medium with different thermodynamic properties. The medium does not create the droplet — it permits it. The conditions of the medium determine what can cohere within it.
- [The Music of the Medium](https://github.com/motebit/motebit/blob/main/THE_MUSIC_OF_THE_MEDIUM.md): their conversation. Pressure waves propagate through Liquescentia and reach the droplet's surface tension boundary. The boundary transduces. Surface tension becomes an instrument.
- [Motebit: The Sovereign Interior](https://github.com/motebit/motebit/blob/main/THE_SOVEREIGN_INTERIOR.md): what is inside. The Ed25519 keypair as internal cohesion; identity, memory, trust, governance as interior structures persisting across mediums and providers.
- [The Metabolic Principle](https://github.com/motebit/motebit/blob/main/THE_METABOLIC_PRINCIPLE.md): what the interior builds vs. what it absorbs. Build the vessel, not the ocean. Enzymes are owned; glucose is adapted. The architectural foundation of every adapter boundary.
- [The Emergent Interior](https://github.com/motebit/motebit/blob/main/THE_EMERGENT_INTERIOR.md): the interior is not passive substance but ongoing process. The droplet is alive between user prompts: it consolidates memory, prunes phantom beliefs, resolves contradictions on its own.
- [Motebit: The Self-Signing Body](https://github.com/motebit/motebit/blob/main/THE_SELF_SIGNING_BODY.md): verifiability that survives the relay. The proof of the interior's private work, mechanically constructed so anyone can verify it without trusting any motebit-operated system.
- [Motebit: The Actor Principle](https://github.com/motebit/motebit/blob/main/THE_ACTOR_PRINCIPLE.md): what motebits are to each other. Every motebit is an actor; the relay is its broker; the receipt chain is its causal log; the adjudicator is its supervisor. The pattern crystallized the moment two motebits exchanged their first task.
- [The Conference of Agents](https://github.com/motebit/motebit/blob/main/THE_CONFERENCE.md): many motebits in conversation. The multi-droplet extension of principles that derive a single droplet.

## Open specification

- [motebit/identity@1.0](https://github.com/motebit/motebit/blob/main/spec/identity-v1.md): the open specification for agent identity files. Apache-2.0.
- [motebit/execution-ledger@1.0](https://github.com/motebit/motebit/blob/main/spec/execution-ledger-v1.md): the signed-receipt ledger every motebit emits. Apache-2.0.
- [motebit/relay-federation@1.0](https://github.com/motebit/motebit/blob/main/spec/relay-federation-v1.md): bilateral peering between independent relays. Apache-2.0.
- All twenty-one specs are in [`spec/`](https://github.com/motebit/motebit/tree/main/spec).

## Published packages

- [`create-motebit`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/create-motebit) — scaffold a signed identity or runnable agent service. Apache-2.0.
- [`@motebit/protocol`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@motebit/protocol) — types, semirings, routing primitives, crypto-suite registry. Apache-2.0.
- [`@motebit/sdk`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@motebit/sdk) — developer contract; stable types, adapter interfaces. Apache-2.0.
- [`@motebit/crypto`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@motebit/crypto) — sign and verify every motebit artifact. Cryptosuite-agile. Apache-2.0.
- [`@motebit/verifier`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@motebit/verifier), [`@motebit/verify`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@motebit/verify), [`@motebit/crypto-{appattest,android-keystore,tpm,webauthn}`](https://www.npmjs.com/org/motebit) — verification libraries. Apache-2.0. (Plus deprecated [`@motebit/crypto-play-integrity`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@motebit/crypto-play-integrity) for one minor cycle.)
- [`motebit`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/motebit) — reference runtime and operator console. BSL-1.1, converging to Apache-2.0 four years after release.

## Optional

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/motebit/motebit): source, issues, discussions. Skippable for narrow context — the docs site and foundational documents above carry the load.
